div.p: If shell calls tcsetpgrp() while NOT in foreground, kernel sends SIGTTOU. Solution: signal(SIGTTOU, SIG_IGN) before tcsetpgrp() calls. This prevents shell from being stopped when reclaiming terminal.

ol.li: setpgid() MUST happen before tcsetpgrp() — PGID must exist before we can set it as foreground
Both parent AND child call setpgid() — Avoids race condition
SIGTTOU must be ignored — Shell needs to call tcsetpgrp() even if not currently foreground

div.p: Creates new process group Job's PGID = child's PID Child becomes group leader
Transfer terminal control Foreground PGID = job_pgid SIGTTOU sent if not foreground
Reclaim terminal control Restore shell as foreground Ignore SIGTTOU during call
Restore terminal settings (job may have changed them)
